references.name: Nothing Technology
references.takeaway: Use a transparent-layer design language where UI surfaces appear as clear glass panels with visible structural elements (grids, wires, dots) beneath them, like a see-through phone back.

elements.description: The top bar uses backdrop-filter blur creating a frosted glass effect where the ambient canvas orbs are visible through the header, giving a transparent-layer feel.
The command palette modal has a blurred backdrop that reveals the underlying page content through a semi-transparent overlay, channeling the see-through aesthetic.
Tooltips use backdrop-filter blur to create a glass-panel appearance floating above the surface, with visible structural border lines beneath.
The ambient canvas with drifting chrome orbs acts as a 'visible structural element' layer beneath all UI surfaces, similar to seeing internal components through a transparent phone back.

references.name: Teenage Engineering
references.takeaway: Present content items as precision-machined components with technical specification overlays — measurement lines, material callouts, and part-number labels that make each element feel engineered.

elements.description: Schema planner entities display fields with PK/FK key badges and type annotations (uuid, varchar, decimal, timestamp), mimicking technical specification callouts on engineered components.
Directory tree files display file sizes in monospaced type (4.2 KB, 12.3 KB), treating code files as precision components with specification metadata.

Workspace panel metrics display financial data with precise decimal formatting ($847,231,400, 18.4%, 2.3x) in monospaced type, creating a technical readout aesthetic reminiscent of hardware spec overlays.
Dashboard gauge cards use circular bezels with metallic borders that evoke precision-machined instrument gauges, with numerical readouts centered inside.
Kanban cards show deal amounts in monospaced type with currency formatting ($15M ask), treating each deal card as a component with a specification label.

references.name: Bang & Olufsen
references.takeaway: Apply metallic gradient fills to card surfaces and interactive elements — linear gradients from dark-grey to light-grey that simulate brushed aluminum when combined with a subtle noise texture.

elements.description: The primary chrome gradient token (--chrome-light) uses a multi-stop linear gradient from light zinc through mid-grey to dark grey and back, simulating brushed aluminum across headings, logos, avatars, and gauge bezels.

The chrome surface gradient (--chrome-surface) provides a darker metallic treatment for table headers, kanban column headers, and settings group headers, simulating anodized aluminum with side lighting.

The chrome sheen sweep (--chrome-sheen) is a diagonal gradient with a bright center band that slides across card surfaces on hover, replicating the light-catching behavior of polished metal under side lighting.
elements.cssProperty: background: linear-gradient(105deg, transparent 0%, rgba(228,228,231,0.08) 45%, rgba(228,228,231,0.15) 50%, rgba(228,228,231,0.08) 55%, transparent 100%)

elements.description: Toggle thumbs in their checked state use a violet-to-light-violet gradient, applying the same metallic fill language to interactive controls.

The overall dark gallery aesthetic — dark charcoal backgrounds (#18181b) with elements emerging from darkness and lit by metallic highlights — directly channels Bang & Olufsen's product-on-void presentation style.
elements.location: Global body background and surface treatment
